explanation: This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (!state_is_MANEUVER & !state_is_TRANSITION & state_is_NOMINAL) and the sensor data is not good (!good), then in the next state the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state (X state_is_MANEUVER).  The chosen template ensures that this transition happens immediately after the condition is met. N_DURATION is set to 1 to reflect the immediate transition.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state within N_DURATION time units if the sensor data is not good.  bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in NOMINAL mode, and bool_exp3 represents the condition of transitioning to MANEUVER mode when sensor data is bad. The formula uses the G operator to globally assert the implication and the F operator to check for the eventual fulfillment of the state change.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, it transitions to the MANEUVER state in the next time step if the sensor data is not good.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in the NOMINAL state, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that sensor data is not good. The chosen template ensures that this transition happens in the next step (X bool_exp3) and is globally true (G).

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, if the sensor data is not good, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state within N_DURATION time units.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL). The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the condition that sensor data is not good (!good). The formula G(bool_exp2 -> F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) ensures that whenever bool_exp2 is true, eventually within N_DURATION time units bool_exp3 will be true.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state (!state_is_MANEUVER & !state_is_TRANSITION & state_is_NOMINAL), and the sensor data is not good (!good), then in the next state (X), the autopilot will transition to the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER). It uses template 60 because it's a simple implication from a condition to the next state's result. N_DURATION is not needed for this simple implication.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state within N_DURATION time units if the sensor data is not good.  bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in NOMINAL mode and !good indicates bad sensor data.  bool_exp3 is the condition of being in MANEUVER mode. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally, and F ensures that eventually the MANEUVER state will be reached.

The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to indicate that the state change must happen within the next state.

The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally, meaning it must be true in all states. N_DURATION is set to 1 to capture the change in the next step.

The formula uses the implication operator to express the conditional relationship between the two states.
